applicable_version: Windows Server 2008 and later where the audit subcategory is enabled
name: A scheduled task was disabled
definition: Windows Security Event ID 4701 records that a scheduled task was disabled.
trigger_scenarios: Microsoft documents that this event is generated every time a scheduled task is disabled.

key_fields.explanation: The disabled task path. Microsoft recommends monitoring critical tasks that should never be disabled.
Task XML content that can identify the disabled command, trigger, and principal.
Hexadecimal logon session for the account disabling the task.

false_positives: Maintenance windows may disable noisy or deprecated scheduled tasks.
Endpoint management software can temporarily disable vendor tasks during updates.

attck_mapping.technique_name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools

detection_notes: Use threshold=1 for Task Name values tied to backups, EDR, logging, or vulnerability scanning that should never be disabled. Microsoft recommends monitoring 4701 for critical tasks; disabling a defensive scheduled task with Task Content pointing to C:\Program Files\SecurityAgent or a backup script is T1562.001 Disable or Modify Tools. Correlate Subject Logon ID with 4624/4688 and look for adjacent 4699 deletion or 4702 update.
kql_snippet:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4701
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, TaskName, TaskContent
spl_snippet: index=wineventlog EventCode=4701 | table _time, host, Account_Name, Task_Name, Task_Content
sample_log:
EventID: 4701
Subject Logon ID: 0x42a91
Task Name: \SecurityAgent\DailyScan
Task Content: <Command>C:\Program Files\SecurityAgent\scan.exe</Command>

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Security Event ID 4701
queries.query:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4701
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, EventID, RenderedDescription
| order by TimeGenerated desc
assumptions: The Windows Security Events connector populates SecurityEvent.
Map raw XML fields to the event-specific fields described on this page.

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Security Event ID 4701
queries.query:
index=wineventlog source="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4701
| table _time, host, EventCode, Message
assumptions: Index, source, and parsed field names vary by deployment.
Apply the documented field conditions after validating local field extraction.

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Security Event ID 4701
assumptions: The Windows Security channel is ingested into Cortex XDR.
Inspect raw provider fields because parsed names vary by collection path.

queries.title: Windows Security Event ID 4701
queries.query:
title: Windows Security Event ID 4701
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: security
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 4701
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative or system activity
level: informational
assumptions: This rule retrieves the event and is not a standalone malicious-behavior detection.
Add the event-specific conditions and correlations documented on this page.

value_references.security_relevance: Interpret EventID with the other documented fields and correlated events; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

value_references.meaning: The full scheduled-task path that was disabled.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret TaskName with the other documented fields and correlated events; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

faqs.question: What does Windows Event ID 4701 mean?
faqs.answer: An enabled scheduled task was disabled.

faqs.question: Does Event ID 4701 always indicate malicious activity?
faqs.answer: No. Task retirement and maintenance can be legitimate.

faqs.question: How should Event ID 4701 be investigated?
faqs.answer: Determine whether the task provides security, backup, or logging functions and correlate SubjectLogonId with process activity.

sources.title: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools (T1562.001)
